segments.text: let not many of you become teachers, knowing that we shall receive a stricter judgment.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: For we all stumble in many things.
If anyone does not stumble in word, he is a perfect man, able also to bridle the whole body.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: Indeed, we put bits in horses' mouths that they may obey us, and we turn their whole body.
Look also at ships: although they are so large and are driven by fierce winds, they are turned by a very small rudder wherever the pilot desires.
Even so the tongue is a little member and boasts great things.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: See how great a forest a little fire kindles!
And the tongue is a fire, a world of iniquity. The tongue is so set among our members that it defiles the whole body, and sets on fire the course of nature; and it is set on fire by hell.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: For every kind of beast and bird, of reptile and creature of the sea, is tamed and has been tamed by mankind.
But no man can tame the tongue. It is an unruly evil, full of deadly poison.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: With it we bless our God and Father, and with it we curse men, who have been made in the similitude of God.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: Out of the same mouth proceed blessing and cursing. My brethren, these things ought not to be so.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: Does a spring send forth fresh water and bitter from the same opening?
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: Can a fig tree, my brethren, bear olives, or a grapevine bear figs? Thus no spring yields both salt water and fresh.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: Who is wise and understanding among you? Let him show by good conduct that his works are done in the meekness of wisdom.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: But if you have bitter envy and self-seeking in your hearts, do not boast and lie against the truth.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: This wisdom does not descend from above, but is earthly, sensual, demonic.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: For where envy and self-seeking exist, confusion and every evil thing are there.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceable, gentle, willing to yield, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality and without hypocrisy.
segments.speaker: James

segments.text: Now the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.
segments.speaker: James
